description: Canonical go2cs symbol table - the SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH for the naming/marker
constants shared between the Go converter and the C# runtime/analyzer. This file is
kept PURE ASCII: every non-ASCII character is written as a JSON \uXXXX escape so no
tool can mangle the glyphs. Generated projections (committed; regenerate with
`go generate .` from src/go2cs, or run src/check-symbol-sync.ps1):
- src/core/go2cs/Symbols.cs  (C#, class go2cs.Symbols - only symbols with
csharp:true; shared into golib and the go2cs-gen analyzer via go2cs.projitems)
Fields: name (identifier, same in both languages); value (the constant string);
csharp (emit into Symbols.cs - keep minimal, only what a C# consumer actually
needs); goEscape (render the Go value with \uXXXX escapes instead of literal
glyphs); goExpr (optional Go initializer expression - a `A + B` concatenation of
previously defined symbol names, validated against value); comment (lines emitted
above the const in BOTH projections); goComment/csComment (language-specific extra
lines); goTrailing (Go end-of-line comment); csBlankBefore (blank separator line
before the entry in Symbols.cs); note (JSON-only documentation, never emitted).

symbols.name: RootNamespace
symbols.goTrailing: Root namespace for all converted C# code
symbols.note: Root C# namespace all converted Go code is emitted into.

symbols.name: PackageSuffix
symbols.goTrailing: Suffix of a package's static partial class (e.g. `fmt_package`)
symbols.note: Static partial class suffix for a Go package's declarations; also consumed by the go2cs-gen analyzer to strip/derive package names.

symbols.name: PackageInfoFileName
symbols.note: Name of the per-package metadata file the converter emits.

symbols.name: PointerPrefix
comment: Extended Unicode characters are being used to help avoid conflicts with Go identifiers for
symbols, markers, intermediate and temporary variables. These characters have to be valid
C# identifiers, i.e., Unicode letter characters, decimal digit characters, connecting
characters, combining characters, or formatting characters. Some character variants will
be better suited to different fonts or display environments. Defaults have been chosen
based on better appearance with common Visual Studio code fonts, e.g., "Cascadia Mono".
symbols.note: U+0436 - pointer marker; composes the golib heap-box type and pointer-adapter names.

symbols.name: TypeAliasDot
symbols.note: U+A4F8 - dot stand-in inside type-alias identifiers.

symbols.name: EllipsisOperator
symbols.note: U+A4F8 x3 - rendering of the Go `...` variadic marker.

symbols.name: OpaqueTrueMarker
symbols.goTrailing: golib static readonly true - NOT compiler-foldable (leading constant-true case, CS8120)
symbols.note: TrueMarker doubled - golib static readonly true, opaque to C# compile-time constant folding (converter-only).

symbols.name: ValueAdapterInfix
csComment: Composes VALUE-form foreign interface adapter class names ({Struct}ᴠ{Iface}) - the
value sibling of the ж-composed pointer adapters (wraps a COPY, Go value semantics).
symbols.note: U+1D20 - value-form foreign interface adapter infix.

symbols.name: ChannelLeftOp
csComment: Channel operator method names - the Go-visual send/receive member forms the converter
emits ("ch.ᐸꟷ(val)" for "ch <- val", "ch.ꟷᐳ(out val)" for "val := <-ch"); the named
channel wrapper template forwards these to its underlying channel<T>.
symbols.note: U+1438 + U+A7F7 - channel send method name.

symbols.name: ChannelRightOp
symbols.note: U+A7F7 + U+1433 - channel receive method name.

symbols.name: PointerDerefOp
symbols.note: C# operator used for pointer dereference (converter-only; golib defines `operator ~` on the heap box).

symbols.name: NilSafeDerefAccessor
goComment: NilSafeDerefAccessor is the golib ж<T> extension method used in place of `.Value` to re-alias a
deref'd pointer parameter that is walked to a nil terminator (see nilSafePtrParamNames). Unlike
`.Value` (which throws on a nil box), it returns a ref to a shared default(T) slot when the box is
nil — the ref is never read while the box is nil, so the standard `*p` panic semantics are
preserved (a genuine nil deref still uses `~`/`.Value`). Includes `()` as it is a method call.
symbols.note: golib pointer-box extension used instead of `.Value` for nil-walked pointer params (converter-only).
